setupGuide.notes: Placeholder for Melbourne Grand Prix Circuit - 57 laps / 302.3 km reproduced as 94 laps of Adelaide Modern. Modern Adelaide keeps the Australian parkland-circuit character (same stand-in as the f1-2000 and f1-2006 packs).

Placeholder for Sepang International Circuit - 56 laps / 310.4 km reproduced as 68 laps of the modern Hockenheim GP. Both are Hermann Tilke designs; the redesigned Hockenheim GP keeps Sepang's long-straight-into-hairpin rhythm (same stand-in as the f1-2006 pack).

Placeholder for Bahrain International Circuit - 57 laps / 308.5 km reproduced as 67 laps of the modern Hockenheim GP. AMS2 has no Bahrain; the redesigned Tilke Hockenheim GP is a same-era, same-designer wide-modern-GP stand-in (same stand-in as the f1-2006 pack).

track.realVenue: Autodromo Internazionale Enzo e Dino Ferrari

setupGuide.notes: AMS2's Imola 2005 is the actual 2005 configuration; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept.

No 2005 Barcelona layout in AMS2; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the era-close pre-2007 layout. BAR (Button/Sato) served the second of its two-race exclusion here, so the grid is two cars lighter.

AMS2's modern Azure Circuit matches the 2005 (post-2004 reprofile) Monaco layout; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept. BAR (Button/Sato) was excluded from this round, so the grid is two cars lighter.
The 2005 European GP ran the Nürburgring GP-Strecke; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the modern GP layout.
2005 Montreal is the same venue as Montreal Modern, so the historical lap count is kept.

The 2005 US GP ran the Indianapolis road course; AMS2's Indianapolis 2001 road course is that same layout, so the historical lap count is kept. (The pack reproduces the scheduled 73-lap race; the actual event was reduced to a 6-car race after the Michelin-tyre withdrawal, which the pack does not model.)

Placeholder for Circuit de Nevers Magny-Cours - 70 laps / 308.8 km reproduced as 74 laps of Le Mans Circuit Bugatti. Keeps the French GP at a French Grand Prix venue (same stand-in as the f1-2000, f1-1988 and f1-2006 packs).
No 2005 Silverstone in AMS2; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the near-identical 2001 layout.

The 2005 German GP ran the modern (2002+) Hockenheim GP; AMS2's Hockenheim GP is that same layout, so the historical lap count is kept. Friesacher's Minardi #20 seat passed to the unbindable Robert Doornbos from this round, so the grid is one car lighter.
AMS2 ships the 2025 Hungaroring; the venue is real and largely unchanged in character since 1986, so the historical lap count is kept.

Placeholder for Istanbul Park - 58 laps / 309.6 km reproduced as 66 laps of Barcelona GP. AMS2 has no Istanbul; the flowing Tilke-designed Barcelona GP is a same-era stand-in with a comparable high-speed-corner character (same stand-in as the f1-2006 pack).
AMS2's Monza 2005 is the 2005 layout; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept.

AMS2 ships Spa-Francorchamps 2005 - the exact 2005 layout; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept. Heidfeld's Williams #8 seat passed to the unbindable Antonio Pizzonia from this round, so the grid is one car lighter.
No 2005 Interlagos layout in AMS2; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the era-close 1993 layout.
Suzuka appears in AMS2 as Kansai; the venue is real, so the historical lap count is kept on the GP layout.

Placeholder for Shanghai International Circuit - 56 laps / 305.3 km reproduced as 65 laps of Barcelona GP. AMS2 has no Shanghai; the Tilke-designed Barcelona GP is a same-era wide-modern-GP stand-in (same stand-in as the f1-2006 pack).
